data_in.description: Microarray expression matrix and phenotype metadata from a public GEO accession

data_out.description: MArrayLM fitted-model object containing coefficient estimates, standard errors, and design matrix information

domain_knowledge: The lmFit function in limma fits ordinary least-squares linear models to microarray or RNA-seq log-expression data row-wise, with each gene treated as a separate response variable.
The design matrix must have full column rank and should encode all experimental conditions, batch effects, or covariates of interest to ensure identifiable coefficient estimates.
Empirical Bayesian methods in limma stabilize variance estimates across genes when the number of arrays is small, improving downstream statistical power in differential expression testing.
The MArrayLM object output by lmFit contains coefficients, standard errors, and residuals that serve as inputs to empirical Bayes functions such as eBayes for shrinkage and moderated t-tests.

direct_checks: verify that a GEO accession identifier or public microarray dataset URL is retrievable and contains valid expression matrix data
verify that limma package (from github:bioc__limma or CRAN) can be loaded in R environment
verify that lmFit function executes without error when passed (i) a valid expression matrix or ExpressionSet object as input, (ii) a design matrix of appropriate dimensions
verify that lmFit returns an object of class MArrayLM containing at least the following fields: coefficients (matrix), stdev.unscaled (matrix), sigma (numeric vector)
verify that coefficients matrix has dimensions matching (number of probes/genes) × (number of design matrix columns)
verify that stdev.unscaled and sigma have lengths and dimensions consistent with the fitted model
verify that no NA or NaN values appear in coefficients matrix where data were present in input expression matrix (robust to biological zero-inflation)
expert_review: confirm that design matrix specification is appropriate for the experimental design of the chosen GEO dataset (e.g., matches sample groupings, block structure, or paired design if applicable)

confirm that coefficient estimates and standard errors are statistically interpretable (e.g., standard errors are positive, coefficients do not show implausible magnitudes relative to the scale of expression values)
confirm that the MArrayLM object is suitable as input for downstream empirical Bayes shrinkage functions (e.g., eBayes) without requiring additional transformation or filtering

[methods] MArrayLM fitted-model object containing coefficient estimates, standard errors, and design matrix information: 'The linear model and differential expression functions apply to a wide variety of gene expression technologies including microarrays'

[discussion] No changelog documenting version history, bug fixes, or API changes for limma is available in the provided documentation: '_No changelog found._'

cmd: library(limma); library(GEOquery); expr_data <- read.csv('{inputs.expression_data}', row.names=1); design <- model.matrix(~1, data=data.frame(sample=colnames(expr_data))); fit <- lmFit(as.matrix(expr_data), design); saveRDS(fit, 'marray_lm_fit.rds'); cat('MArrayLM object saved to marray_lm_fit.rds\n'); stopifnot(!is.null(fit$coefficients), nrow(fit$coefficients) > 0, ncol(fit$coefficients) == ncol(design), all(fit$stdev.unscaled > 0 | is.finite(fit$stdev.unscaled)))

expected_outputs: MArrayLM fitted-model object containing coefficient estimates, standard errors, and design matrix information
finding: The limma package applies linear model functions to microarray technologies for analyzing designed experiments and assessing differential expression.
inputs: Microarray expression matrix and phenotype metadata from a public GEO accession
methodology_summary: Load microarray expression matrix and sample metadata from a public GEO repository.
Construct a design matrix encoding experimental groups, conditions, and any batch or covariate effects.
Apply limma's lmFit function to fit row-wise linear models across all genes using the design matrix.
Retrieve the MArrayLM object containing coefficient estimates, standard errors, and model diagnostics.

Validation: Confirm that the MArrayLM object contains a non-empty coefficients matrix with dimensions matching the number of genes and design matrix columns, and that all standard error estimates are positive and finite.
missing_information: No changelog documenting version history, bug fixes, or API changes for limma is available in the provided documentation

task_description: Fit a linear model to a publicly deposited microarray dataset using limma's lmFit function with a specified design matrix, producing a MArrayLM object containing coefficient estimates and standard errors for subsequent empirical Bayes shrinkage analysis.

task_inputs.label: Microarray expression matrix and phenotype metadata

task_objective: Fit a linear model to a GEO microarray dataset using limma's lmFit function with a design matrix and extract the resulting MArrayLM object containing coefficient estimates and standard errors.

task_outputs.label: MArrayLM fitted-model object with coefficients and standard errors

title: Reconstruct empirical Bayes moderated t-statistic computation via limma's eBayes

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: MArrayLM fitted-model object containing coefficient estimates, standard errors, and design matrix information.
workflow_description: 1. Load microarray expression data and phenotype metadata from a public GEO accession using appropriate R data-loading functions. 2. Construct a design matrix encoding the experimental groups or conditions of interest. 3. Apply limma's lmFit function to fit a linear model to the expression matrix using the design matrix, obtaining coefficient estimates and residual standard errors. 4. Retrieve and inspect the resulting MArrayLM object to verify that all contrast coefficients and standard errors have been computed.
